case_title: JAMES N LEMON VS. RAISER SENIOR SERVICES et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 301, *A R G U E D AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING -- DEFENDANT RAISER SENIOR SERVICES, RAISER RESOURCES, THE J.H.R. TRUST AND JENNIFER RAISER'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT -- RE: CAUSES OF ACTION, 2,3,10 & 11--SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. RE: CAUSE OF ACTION 5--SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS DUPLICATIVE OF C/A 6. RE: CAUSE OF ACTION 1--SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO RECAST AS B OF K CLAIM. RE: CAUSE OF ACTION 12--SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD BREACH. RE: CAUSES OF ACTION 4,6,7--OVERRULED. 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO CONFORM COMPLAINT TO RULING. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Glenda M. Zarbock, Esq., Hanson, Bridgett, 415-777-3200, for defendant/moving party Raiser Senior Services.
Edward Napier Thomson, Esq., 415-332-2670, for plaintiffs/opposing parties James N. Lemon and all other plaintiffs named in 1st amended complaint.
*A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- DEFENDANT RAISER SENIOR SERVICES, RAISER RESOURCES, THE J.H.R. TRUST and JENNIFER RAISER's DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT --
Re: causes of action, 2,3,10 & 11--sustained without leave to amend.
Re: cause of action 5--sustained without leave to amend as duplicative of c/a 6.
Re: cause of action 1--sustained with leave to amend to recast as B of K claim.
Re: cause of action 12--sustained with leave to amend to plead breach.
re: causes of action 4,6,7--overruled.
10 days leave to amend to conform complaint to ruling. Reporter: Joseph Vickstein, CSR # 4780 (DAG/301/EH)
